CohortPipe.description: Time-based and behavioral cohort analysis, retention, conversion, and LTV metrics.

form_time_cohorts.description: Form cohorts based on time periods
form_time_cohorts.usage_description: Groups users/customers by their sign-up, registration, or first activity date. Common use cases: tracking retention by sign-up month, analyzing quarterly cohorts, creating weekly purchase cohorts, and grouping customers by joining year for annual spending analysis. Supports daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly periods.
example_questions: How do I group customers by their sign-up month?
I want to create cohorts based on when users first registered
How can I analyze customer behavior by joining date?
I need to form time-based cohorts for retention analysis

form_behavioral_cohorts.description: Form cohorts based on user behavior
form_behavioral_cohorts.usage_description: Creates user segments based on behavioral characteristics like spending levels, activity frequency, or feature usage. Useful for analyzing high-value vs low-value customers, power users vs casual users, and creating targeted marketing segments.
example_questions: How do I create customer segments based on spending behavior?
I want to group users by their activity frequency
How can I segment customers by their usage patterns?
I need to create behavioral cohorts for targeted marketing

calculate_retention.usage_description: Measures how many users from each cohort return over time. Essential for understanding user engagement, product stickiness, and the effectiveness of onboarding processes. Supports classic, rolling, and unbounded retention calculations.
example_questions: How do I calculate customer retention rates by cohort?
I want to measure how many users return over time
How can I analyze user engagement and stickiness?
I need to calculate retention metrics for different user groups

calculate_conversion.description: Calculate conversion funnel metrics for cohorts
calculate_conversion.usage_description: Tracks how users from different cohorts progress through conversion funnels (e.g., signup → activation → first purchase). Helps identify which cohorts have better conversion rates and where users drop off in the funnel.
example_questions: How do I track conversion rates through funnels by cohort?
I want to analyze where users drop off in conversion funnels
How can I measure funnel performance across different user groups?
I need to calculate conversion metrics for signup to purchase flows

calculate_lifetime_value.description: Calculate lifetime value metrics for cohorts
calculate_lifetime_value.usage_description: Estimates the total value each cohort will generate over time. Critical for customer acquisition cost analysis, marketing ROI calculations, and understanding which user segments are most valuable to the business.
example_questions: How do I calculate customer lifetime value by cohort?
I want to estimate the total value each user group will generate
How can I analyze customer acquisition cost vs lifetime value?
I need to calculate LTV metrics for marketing ROI analysis

FunnelPipe.description: User funnel analysis, including time, segment, and path analysis.

analyze_funnel.description: Analyze a user funnel using the existing CohortPipe framework
analyze_funnel.usage_description: Creates conversion funnels to track user progression through defined steps (e.g., view → add to cart → purchase). Essential for identifying drop-off points, optimizing user flows, and measuring funnel performance across different user segments.
example_questions: How do I analyze user conversion funnels?
I want to track user progression through defined steps
How can I identify drop-off points in user flows?
I need to measure funnel performance across user segments

analyze_funnel_by_time.description: Analyze funnel performance over time using the existing CohortPipe framework
analyze_funnel_by_time.usage_description: Tracks how funnel conversion rates change over time, helping identify seasonal patterns, the impact of product changes, and long-term trends in user behavior. Useful for measuring the success of optimization efforts.
example_questions: How do I track funnel performance over time?
I want to see how conversion rates change over time
How can I identify seasonal patterns in funnel performance?
I need to measure the impact of product changes on funnels

analyze_funnel_by_segment.description: Analyze funnel performance across different user segments using the existing CohortPipe framework
analyze_funnel_by_segment.usage_description: Compares funnel performance across different user segments (e.g., by device, location, acquisition channel). Helps identify which segments have better conversion rates and where to focus optimization efforts.
example_questions: How do I compare funnel performance across user segments?
I want to analyze funnels by device, location, or acquisition channel
How can I identify which segments have better conversion rates?
I need to compare funnel performance across different user groups

analyze_user_paths.description: Analyze actual paths users take through the funnel (not just the ideal path)
analyze_user_paths.usage_description: Discovers the actual sequences of actions users take, not just the predefined funnel steps. Reveals unexpected user behaviors, alternative conversion paths, and opportunities for funnel optimization.
example_questions: How do I analyze the actual paths users take through funnels?
I want to discover unexpected user behaviors and alternative paths
How can I find the real user journey sequences?
I need to analyze actual user paths vs ideal funnel steps

get_funnel_summary.description: Return a summary of funnel analysis results
get_funnel_summary.usage_description: Provides a comprehensive overview of funnel performance including conversion rates, drop-off points, and key metrics. Useful for reporting and identifying the most critical areas for improvement.
example_questions: How do I get a summary of funnel analysis results?
I want to see conversion rates and drop-off points
How can I get a comprehensive overview of funnel performance?
I need a summary report of funnel analysis for reporting

compare_segments.usage_description: Statistical comparison of funnel performance between different user segments. Helps identify significant differences in conversion rates and prioritize optimization efforts based on segment performance.
example_questions: How do I statistically compare funnel metrics across segments?
I want to identify significant differences in conversion rates
How can I prioritize optimization efforts based on segment performance?
I need to compare funnel performance between different user groups

KMeansPipe.description: Pipeline-style K-means clustering with functional composition for unsupervised learning.

prepare_clustering_data.description: Prepare and preprocess data for K-means clustering with scaling and outlier handling
prepare_clustering_data.usage_description: Essential preprocessing step that scales features to similar ranges, handles outliers, and prepares data for clustering. Critical for ensuring clustering quality and preventing features with larger scales from dominating the analysis.
example_questions: How do I prepare data for K-means clustering?
I want to scale features and handle outliers before clustering
How can I preprocess data to ensure clustering quality?
I need to prepare data with scaling and outlier handling for clustering

engineer_clustering_features.description: Create advanced features optimized for clustering algorithms
engineer_clustering_features.usage_description: Creates interaction features, polynomial features, ratios, and aggregations that can improve clustering quality. Useful for capturing complex relationships in the data and creating more meaningful customer segments.
example_questions: How do I create advanced features for clustering algorithms?
I want to engineer features that improve clustering quality
How can I create interaction features and ratios for clustering?
I need to build features optimized for customer segmentation

reduce_dimensions.description: Apply dimensionality reduction for visualization and clustering efficiency
reduce_dimensions.usage_description: Reduces the number of features using PCA or t-SNE to improve clustering performance and enable 2D/3D visualization. Essential for high-dimensional data and creating interpretable cluster visualizations.
example_questions: How do I reduce dimensions for clustering visualization?
I want to apply PCA or t-SNE for clustering efficiency
How can I create 2D/3D visualizations of high-dimensional clusters?
I need to reduce dimensions to improve clustering performance

configure_kmeans.usage_description: Sets up K-means parameters including number of clusters, initialization method, and convergence criteria. The core configuration step for customer segmentation, market research, and data exploration.
example_questions: How do I configure K-means clustering parameters?
I want to set up K-means for customer segmentation
How can I configure the number of clusters and initialization method?
I need to configure K-means for market research and data exploration

configure_minibatch_kmeans.description: Configure MiniBatch K-means for large-scale clustering
configure_minibatch_kmeans.usage_description: Optimized version of K-means for large datasets that processes data in mini-batches. Useful for big data applications where memory efficiency and speed are critical.
example_questions: How do I configure MiniBatch K-means for large datasets?
I want to use K-means for big data with memory efficiency
How can I set up MiniBatch K-means for large-scale clustering?
I need to configure K-means for big data applications with speed optimization

fit_kmeans.description: Train K-means model and assign cluster labels
fit_kmeans.usage_description: Executes the clustering algorithm and assigns each data point to a cluster. The main training step that creates the customer segments or data groupings.
example_questions: How do I train a K-means model and assign cluster labels?
I want to execute the clustering algorithm and create customer segments
How can I fit K-means and assign data points to clusters?
I need to train K-means and create data groupings

evaluate_clustering.usage_description: Measures clustering quality using metrics like silhouette score, Calinski-Harabasz score, and Davies-Bouldin score. Essential for validating cluster quality and comparing different clustering configurations.
example_questions: How do I evaluate clustering quality with metrics?
I want to calculate silhouette score and other clustering metrics
How can I validate cluster quality and compare configurations?
I need to measure clustering performance with evaluation metrics

profile_clusters.description: Create detailed statistical profiles of each cluster
profile_clusters.usage_description: Analyzes the characteristics of each cluster to understand what makes them distinct. Critical for interpreting clusters and creating actionable business insights from the segmentation.
example_questions: How do I create statistical profiles of each cluster?
I want to analyze cluster characteristics and understand what makes them distinct
How can I interpret clusters and create actionable business insights?
I need to profile clusters for customer segmentation analysis

find_optimal_k.description: Determine optimal k using elbow method and silhouette analysis
find_optimal_k.usage_description: Automatically determines the best number of clusters using statistical methods. Eliminates guesswork in choosing the number of segments and ensures optimal clustering quality.
example_questions: How do I find the optimal number of clusters automatically?
I want to use elbow method and silhouette analysis to determine k
How can I automatically determine the best number of clusters?
I need to find optimal k using statistical methods for clustering

plot_clusters_2d.description: Visualize clusters in 2D feature space with centroids
plot_clusters_2d.usage_description: Creates scatter plots showing how data points are grouped in 2D space. Essential for understanding cluster boundaries, identifying outliers, and communicating results to stakeholders.
example_questions: How do I visualize clusters in 2D space with centroids?
I want to create scatter plots showing cluster groupings
How can I visualize cluster boundaries and identify outliers?
I need to plot clusters in 2D for stakeholder communication

plot_cluster_profiles.usage_description: Creates heatmaps showing how each cluster differs across features. Useful for understanding cluster characteristics and identifying the most important features for each segment.
example_questions: How do I visualize cluster characteristics using heatmaps?
I want to create heatmaps showing cluster differences across features
How can I identify the most important features for each cluster?
I need to visualize cluster profiles with heatmaps

plot_elbow_curve.description: Plot elbow curve for optimal k selection using within-cluster sum of squares
plot_elbow_curve.usage_description: Visualizes the trade-off between number of clusters and clustering quality. Helps in manually selecting the optimal number of clusters when automatic methods are inconclusive.
example_questions: How do I plot an elbow curve for optimal k selection?
I want to visualize the trade-off between clusters and quality
How can I manually select optimal k using elbow curve?
I need to plot within-cluster sum of squares for k selection

plot_silhouette_scores.description: Plot silhouette scores across different k values for cluster quality assessment
plot_silhouette_scores.usage_description: Shows how well-separated the clusters are for different values of k. Higher silhouette scores indicate better-defined, more separated clusters.
example_questions: How do I plot silhouette scores across different k values?
I want to assess cluster quality using silhouette scores
How can I see how well-separated clusters are for different k?
I need to plot silhouette scores for cluster quality assessment

save_kmeans_model.description: Save trained clustering model with preprocessing pipeline and cluster profiles
save_kmeans_model.usage_description: Persists the trained model for future use, including preprocessing steps and cluster profiles. Essential for production deployment and model versioning.
example_questions: How do I save a trained K-means model for future use?
I want to persist the clustering model with preprocessing pipeline
How can I save cluster profiles and preprocessing steps?
I need to save the model for production deployment and versioning

print_cluster_summary.description: Print comprehensive clustering analysis summary and insights
print_cluster_summary.usage_description: Provides a complete overview of the clustering results including cluster sizes, key characteristics, and business insights. Useful for reporting and stakeholder communication.
example_questions: How do I print a comprehensive clustering analysis summary?
I want to see cluster sizes, characteristics, and business insights
How can I get a complete overview of clustering results?
I need to print clustering summary for reporting and stakeholder communication

MovingAggrPipe.description: Moving window and expanding window metrics, including moving averages, variance, quantiles, and custom aggregations.

moving_average.description: Calculate moving averages for specified columns
moving_average.usage_description: Smooths time series data to identify trends and reduce noise. Common applications: smoothing daily sales data, analyzing website traffic trends, and identifying patterns in sensor readings. Supports simple, weighted, and exponential moving averages.
example_questions: How do I calculate moving averages to smooth time series data?
I want to identify trends in daily sales data using moving averages
How can I reduce noise in sensor readings with moving averages?
I need to smooth website traffic trends using moving averages

moving_variance.description: Calculate moving variance and standard deviation for specified columns
moving_variance.usage_description: Measures volatility and variability over time. Useful for detecting changes in data variability, analyzing risk in financial data, and identifying periods of unusual activity in sensor data.
example_questions: How do I calculate moving variance to measure volatility over time?
I want to detect changes in data variability using moving variance
How can I analyze risk in financial data with moving variance?
I need to identify periods of unusual activity in sensor data

moving_sum.description: Calculate moving sums for specified columns
moving_sum.usage_description: Tracks cumulative totals over rolling windows. Common uses: calculating rolling revenue totals, tracking cumulative user activity, and analyzing running totals in time series data.

I want to calculate rolling revenue totals over time
How can I track cumulative user activity with moving sums?

moving_quantile.usage_description: Provides robust measures of central tendency and spread that are less sensitive to outliers than means. Useful for analyzing data distributions over time and identifying changes in data characteristics.

How can I understand dynamic relationships in financial data?

moving_zscore.usage_description: Standardizes data relative to local windows, making it easier to identify unusual values. Useful for anomaly detection, quality control, and identifying outliers in streaming data.

OperationsPipe.description: Statistical and comparison operations, including percent/absolute change, covariate adjustment, filtering, and power analysis.

PercentChange.description: Compute the percent change (other - baseline) / baseline
PercentChange.usage_description: Calculates percentage differences between groups or time periods. Essential for A/B testing, measuring lift from experiments, comparing performance across segments, and analyzing growth rates. Common use cases: conversion rate improvements, revenue growth analysis, and performance comparisons.
example_questions: How do I compute percent change between groups or time periods?
I want to calculate percentage differences for A/B testing
How can I measure lift from experiments using percent change?
I need to analyze growth rates and performance comparisons

AbsoluteChange.usage_description: Measures absolute differences between groups or time periods. Useful for understanding the magnitude of changes, analyzing absolute improvements, and comparing raw performance differences. Often used alongside percent change for complete analysis.
example_questions: How do I compute absolute change between groups or time periods?
I want to measure the magnitude of changes between groups
How can I analyze absolute improvements in performance?
I need to compare raw performance differences between segments

MH.usage_description: Advanced statistical method for analyzing stratified data and controlling for confounding variables. Essential for complex experimental designs, epidemiological studies, and situations where you need to account for multiple factors simultaneously.
example_questions: How do I compute the Mantel-Haenszel estimator for stratified data?
I want to control for confounding variables in complex experiments
How can I analyze stratified data accounting for multiple factors?
I need to use advanced statistical methods for epidemiological studies

CUPED.description: Compute the absolute change adjusted using the CUPED approach
CUPED.usage_description: Reduces variance in experimental analysis by using pre-treatment covariates. Improves statistical power and precision in A/B testing, making it easier to detect true treatment effects and reducing the required sample size.
example_questions: How do I compute CUPED-adjusted absolute change?
I want to reduce variance in experimental analysis using pre-treatment covariates
How can I improve statistical power in A/B testing with CUPED?
I need to detect true treatment effects with reduced sample size requirements

PrePostChange.description: Compute the percent change adjusted using the PrePost approach
PrePostChange.usage_description: Analyzes changes using pre-treatment data as covariates. Useful for before-after studies, measuring the impact of interventions, and analyzing changes while controlling for baseline differences.
example_questions: How do I compute PrePost-adjusted percent change?
I want to analyze changes using pre-treatment data as covariates
How can I measure the impact of interventions with PrePost analysis?
I need to analyze before-after studies controlling for baseline differences

FilterConditions.description: Filter the data to include only specified conditions
FilterConditions.usage_description: Subsets data to focus on specific groups or conditions. Essential for targeted analysis, comparing specific segments, and ensuring analysis focuses on relevant data subsets.

I want to subset data to focus on specific groups
How can I ensure analysis focuses on relevant data subsets?

PowerAnalysis.usage_description: Calculates required sample sizes for statistical tests. Critical for experimental design, ensuring studies have sufficient power to detect meaningful effects, and optimizing resource allocation in research and testing.

I want to calculate required sample sizes for statistical tests
How can I ensure studies have sufficient power to detect effects?

StratifiedSummary.usage_description: Provides detailed breakdowns of metrics across different groups or strata. Useful for understanding how performance varies across segments, identifying opportunities, and creating comprehensive reports.

BootstrapCI.usage_description: Provides robust confidence intervals without assuming specific data distributions. Essential for reliable statistical inference, especially with non-normal data or complex sampling designs.

SelectPipe.description: Pipeline-style column selection tool with functional composition and selector-based interface similar to dplyr's select functionality.

from_engine.description: Create a SelectPipe from an engine and table name
from_engine.usage_description: Initializes a SelectPipe with a database engine and table name for data operations. Essential for working with database tables, enabling column selection operations on large datasets without loading all data into memory. Common use cases: selecting specific columns from database tables, creating data pipelines with database sources, and performing column operations on remote data.
example_questions: How do I connect to a database table for column selection?

to_df.description: Convert the selection to a DataFrame
to_df.usage_description: Converts the current column selection into a pandas DataFrame. Critical for materializing the selection results, enabling further data analysis, and providing the final output for downstream processing. Essential for completing data pipelines and obtaining the selected data for analysis or export.

Select.description: Select columns using one or more selectors
Select.usage_description: Applies column selection using various selector types (name-based, pattern-based, type-based, etc.). Core function for column selection operations, enabling flexible and powerful column selection strategies. Essential for data preprocessing, feature selection, and creating focused datasets for analysis.

How can I pick columns based on their names or types?

Deselect.description: Remove columns using one or more selectors
Deselect.usage_description: Removes columns from the current selection using various selector types. Useful for excluding unwanted columns, removing sensitive data, and refining column selections. Essential for data cleaning and creating focused datasets by removing irrelevant columns.
example_questions: How do I remove unwanted columns from my selection?
I want to exclude certain columns from my dataset
How can I drop columns that I don't need?
I need to remove sensitive or irrelevant columns from my selection

Rename.usage_description: Renames columns in the current selection using a mapping of old names to new names. Critical for standardizing column names, improving readability, and preparing data for downstream analysis. Essential for data preparation and ensuring consistent naming conventions.
example_questions: How do I rename columns in my dataset?
I want to change column names to make them more readable
How can I standardize column naming conventions?
I need to rename columns for better data preparation

Reorder.usage_description: Reorders columns in the current selection according to a specified order. Useful for organizing data presentation, grouping related columns together, and creating logical column arrangements. Essential for data organization and improving data readability.

I want to arrange columns in a specific sequence
How can I organize columns in a logical order?
I need to reorder columns for better data presentation

AddColumns.description: Add new computed columns to the selection
AddColumns.usage_description: Adds new columns to the selection, either with constant values or computed from existing columns using functions. Essential for feature engineering, creating derived metrics, and adding calculated fields to datasets. Critical for data transformation and creating new analytical variables.

Useful for focusing analysis on quantitative variables, preparing data for statistical analysis, and selecting features for machine learning models. Essential for data type-based filtering.

select_strings.usage_description: Convenience function that selects all string/text columns from the dataset. Useful for text analysis, categorical variable selection, and focusing on qualitative data. Essential for text processing pipelines and categorical data analysis.

select_dates.usage_description: Convenience function that selects all temporal columns (datetime, date, time) from the dataset. Useful for time series analysis, temporal feature selection, and date-based data processing. Essential for time-based analysis and temporal data preparation.

select_by_pattern.description: Select columns matching a regex pattern
select_by_pattern.usage_description: Selects columns whose names match a specified regular expression pattern. Useful for selecting columns with similar naming patterns, batch column selection, and pattern-based data organization. Essential for systematic column selection and naming convention-based filtering.

select_by_prefix.description: Select columns starting with a prefix
select_by_prefix.usage_description: Selects columns whose names start with a specified prefix. Useful for selecting related columns (e.g., all columns starting with 'user_'), organizing data by categories, and systematic column grouping. Essential for hierarchical data organization.
example_questions: How do I select columns that start with a specific prefix?

select_by_suffix.description: Select columns ending with a suffix
select_by_suffix.usage_description: Selects columns whose names end with a specified suffix. Useful for selecting columns with similar purposes (e.g., all columns ending with '_count'), organizing data by function, and systematic column filtering. Essential for functional data organization.
example_questions: How do I select columns that end with a specific suffix?

select_containing.usage_description: Selects columns whose names contain a specified substring. Useful for flexible column selection, finding related columns, and partial name matching. Essential for exploratory column selection and flexible data filtering.

preview_selection.description: Preview what columns would be selected without creating a pipe
preview_selection.usage_description: Shows what columns would be selected by given selectors without actually applying the selection. Useful for testing selection logic, understanding selector behavior, and planning column selection strategies. Essential for selection validation and planning.
